archetype: ARCHETYPE-2 (Integration) for `PrismaSagaStore` (wraps Prisma/Postgres behind the existing `SagaStorePort`, no new public port) + ARCHETYPE-5 (Plugin Package) for the schema contribution, runtime wiring, and barrel re-exports in `@netscript/plugin-sagas`. Core `@netscript/plugin-sagas-core` stays platform-neutral (no Prisma dep); the concrete adapter lives in the plugin alongside `KvSagaStore`, mirroring the #74 placement and the `@netscript/queue` multi-adapter precedent.
scopeOverlays: SCOPE-service (composition-root backend selection in plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts + standalone saga-supervisor)
CLI touch (@netscript/cli saga scaffold backend option — additive prompt/flag + generated wiring)
lockedDecisions: Delivery = fast-follow ADDITIVE slice; #74 KvSagaStore + SagaStorePort untouched. No port change.

`PrismaSagaStore implements SagaStorePort` in plugin layer (plugins/sagas/src/runtime/prisma-saga-store.ts), peer to KvSagaStore. Receives a host-provided PrismaClient (ctx.db.getClient() / @netscript/database) — consumer-brings-the-instance; the store does not own connection lifecycle.

Both backends first-class, EXPLICIT selection (no silent default in the multi-backend entrypoint): env NETSCRIPT_SAGA_STORE=kv|prisma, appsettings sagas.store.backend, and a @netscript/cli scaffold option. Existing zero-arg createDurableSagaRuntime() retains KV for current internal callers (back-compat); scaffold output always passes an explicit backend.

Dedicated durable Prisma runtime tables (saga_runtime_state keyed by instanceId; saga_runtime_transition (instanceId,version); saga_runtime_correlation unique(sagaId,correlationKey)), SEPARATE from the existing read-model/projection tables (SagaInstance/SagaExecutionHistory) which #74 framed as projection. Rationale: port keys on instanceId alone vs projection PK [sagaName,id]; keeps durable-write and read-model concerns distinct.

Optimistic write parity: save(envelope,{expectedVersion}) via $transaction updateMany where {instanceId,version:expectedVersion}; count===0 && rowExists -> SagasError.validationFailed('Saga store version mismatch for <instanceId>') byte-identical to KvSagaStore; first write -> create. delete -> single $transaction over state+transitions+correlation.

Seam refactor (backward-compatible): createDurableSagaRuntime gains backend?/prisma? options, opens Deno.Kv ONLY for the KV backend, returns dispose() (kv optional, present only for KV). saga-supervisor.ts and services/src/main.ts call durable.dispose() instead of durable.kv.close().
Deps via catalog only: @prisma/client ^7.8.0 (already cataloged) + @netscript/database (already a dep). No new third-party dep into core. No version-pin / de-catalog changes.
Prisma SagaIdempotencyPort parity (#75 idempotency port) is EXPLICITLY DEFERRED to a possible fast-follow; recorded in arch-debt.md, not silently dropped. KV remains the idempotency backend.
Scaffold output changes -> PR carries the e2e-cli-gate label and `deno task e2e:cli run scaffold.runtime` runs at eval.
contracts: SagaStorePort (packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/ports/saga-store-port.ts:23) — REUSED unchanged; PrismaSagaStore is its second production implementor.
PrismaSagaStore class + PrismaSagaStoreOptions ({ prisma: PrismaClient; now?: () => Date }) + optional close()/entries()/transitions() diagnostic parity — new plugin runtime surface.

createDurableSagaRuntime(options?) extended: DurableSagaRuntimeOptions gains backend?: 'kv'|'prisma' and prisma?: PrismaClient; return type gains dispose(): Promise<void> and makes kv optional — backward-compatible superset.
sagas.prisma: 3 new dedicated durable models (saga_runtime_state / saga_runtime_transition / saga_runtime_correlation); existing projection models unchanged.
@netscript/cli saga scaffold: new saga-store-backend option wiring chosen backend into generated main.ts + appsettings — new CLI surface (additive).
testPlan: unit: PrismaSagaStore round-trips SagaStateEnvelope (save->load), correlation save->findByCorrelation, appendTransition ordering, delete clears state+transitions+correlation — over a Prisma test client (pglite/embedded Postgres or transaction-rollback fixture per repo precedent).
unit failure-path: PrismaSagaStore.save with stale expectedVersion rejects with SagasError.validationFailed (parity with KvSagaStore + MemorySagaStore).
unit: createDurableSagaRuntime selects KV vs Prisma by backend/env, does NOT open Deno.Kv for the Prisma backend, returns a working dispose() for each backend.
integration cross-restart durability: two sequential Prisma-backed runtimes over the SAME database resume persisted saga state/version instead of initialState for a correlated message.
regression: existing plugin-sagas-core + plugins/sagas suites stay green (KV path + engine unchanged); saga-supervisor/main teardown still closes resources via dispose().
e2e (runtime): deno task e2e:cli run scaffold.runtime --cleanup --format pretty exercises the scaffold backend option against real Postgres.
planGateSelfCheck: Research present and current: PASS — seam, KvSagaStore, SagaStorePort, sagas.prisma, and ctx.db.getClient() wiring all re-read on the merged umbrella (post-#74). Decisions locked: PASS — 9 locked decisions with rationale (plugin placement, dedicated tables, explicit selection, back-compat seam, catalog deps, deferred idempotency). Open-decision sweep: PASS — the two open items (dedicated-vs-shared tables; explicit-choice-vs-back-compat-default) are surfaced with a recommended resolution for PLAN-EVAL to ratify; idempotency-port deferral is explicit and debt-logged. Commit slices: PASS — 6 ordered slices, each names files + gate. Risk register: PASS — seam-teardown, no-live-DB testing, no-implicit-default-vs-back-compat. Gate set: PASS — ARCHETYPE-2/5 gates, scoped check/test/lint/fmt wrappers, e2e:cli REQUIRED (scaffold changed) with e2e-cli-gate label. jsr-audit: PASS — applied to plugin surface; no core surface change. Residual FAIL_PLAN risk: LOW-MEDIUM — most likely challenge is the explicit-choice vs retained zero-arg KV default reconciliation; plan offers an explicit answer and defers to PLAN-EVAL to harden if required.
openQuestions: RECOMMENDED-RESOLVED: dedicated durable runtime tables vs promoting the projection SagaInstance — plan recommends dedicated tables (PK/namespace fit, keeps #74 projection framing); PLAN-EVAL to ratify or require shared.

RECOMMENDED-RESOLVED: 'no implicit default' vs back-compat — plan retains zero-arg KV default only for existing internal callers while scaffold/docs always present an explicit choice; PLAN-EVAL to confirm or require a hard explicit-choice error everywhere.
DEFERRED-SAFE: Prisma SagaIdempotencyPort parity — separate fast-follow, debt-logged; forces no rework of this slice's store contract.
risks: Seam refactor touches shared teardown (kv.close() -> dispose()); mitigated by keeping kv optional and KV behavior-identical, covered by existing supervisor/runtime tests.

Prisma store unit testing without a live DB; mitigated by pglite/embedded Postgres or transaction-rollback fixture, with full durable-contract assertions also exercised under scaffold.runtime (real Postgres).
Optimistic-write semantics must match KvSagaStore/MemorySagaStore stale-version rejection exactly or engine concurrency tests diverge; covered by parity tests.
Adding a scaffold backend option changes generated output; must run scaffold.runtime e2e and carry the e2e-cli-gate label or CI/process gate is bypassed.
